name: Cream of Asparagus
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon To 1 teaspoon Dill weed (to taste)
recipeInstructions:
1. Break off and discard tough asparagus bottoms.
2. Break off tips; set aside.
3. Coarsely chop the stalks; cook in a skillet with onion, using butter and salting lightly.
4. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes until the onions are clear.
5. Sprinkle with flour and continue stirring over the lowest possible heat for 5 to 8 minutes.
6. Slowly add water or stock, stirring constantly.
7. Cook for an additional 8 to 10 minutes, stirring frequently, until thickened.
8. Cool slightly.
9. In a blender, puree the sauce bit-by-bit with milk until thoroughly smooth.
10. Return the puree to a 3-quart pan (preferably a double boiler).
11. Add dill, salt, pepper, and tamari.
12. Heat gently without boiling.
13. Cook asparagus tips in boiling water for about 2 minutes until tender but still very green; drain.
14. Add the asparagus tips to the soup.
